description: TRIZ Separation Principles for resolving physical contradictions

separation_principles.name: Separation in Time
separation_principles.description: The system satisfies contradictory requirements at different times. The parameter has value A during time period T1 and value NOT-A during time period T2.
when_to_use: The contradictory requirements occur during different phases of operation
The system has distinct operating modes or states
The parameter can be toggled between values

principle_rationale.15: Dynamics — make the object adaptive, changing properties over time
principle_rationale.10: Preliminary Action — perform the change in advance of when it's needed
principle_rationale.11: Beforehand Cushioning — prepare the alternate state before the transition
principle_rationale.16: Partial or Excessive Action — use slightly more or less during the transition window
principle_rationale.19: Periodic Action — alternate between the two states periodically
principle_rationale.20: Continuity of Useful Action — maintain one state continuously until switch is needed
principle_rationale.21: Skipping — rush through the undesirable state at high speed
principle_rationale.34: Discarding and Recovering — dissolve the current state and restore the needed one
principle_rationale.9: Preliminary Anti-action — counter the upcoming state change in advance

software_examples: Feature flags: enabled in testing, disabled in production
Blue-green deployment: old version serves traffic, then instant cutover
Scheduled scaling: more instances during peak hours, fewer during off-peak
Circuit breaker: closed during normal operation, open during failure

separation_principles.name: Separation in Space
separation_principles.description: Different parts of the system satisfy different contradictory requirements. Value A exists in spatial region R1 and value NOT-A in region R2.
when_to_use: Different parts of the system have different operating requirements
The contradictory properties are needed in different locations
The system can be partitioned into zones

principle_rationale.1: Segmentation — divide the system so each segment has the needed property
principle_rationale.2: Taking Out — extract the part that needs the different property
principle_rationale.3: Local Quality — optimize each region for its specific requirement
principle_rationale.4: Asymmetry — use different properties in different spatial directions
principle_rationale.7: Nested Doll — nest one spatial region inside another
principle_rationale.17: Another Dimension — move the separation into a different dimension
principle_rationale.13: The Other Way Around — invert which region has which property
principle_rationale.24: Intermediary — insert a buffer zone between contradictory regions
principle_rationale.30: Flexible Shells — use flexible boundaries between regions

software_examples: Microservices: each service optimized for its own requirement
CDN + origin: cached content at edge, dynamic content at origin
Read replicas: write consistency at primary, read throughput at replicas
Tiered storage: hot data in memory, cold data on disk

separation_principles.name: Separation upon Condition
separation_principles.description: The system satisfies contradictory requirements under different conditions. Value A under condition C1 and value NOT-A under condition C2.
when_to_use: The contradictory requirements depend on external conditions or inputs
The system can sense conditions and adapt its behavior
Different user contexts require different properties

principle_rationale.15: Dynamics — adapt the property based on current conditions
principle_rationale.35: Parameter Changes — change physical or logical state based on conditions
principle_rationale.22: Blessing in Disguise — use the condition itself as the trigger for beneficial change
principle_rationale.23: Feedback — use sensing to detect conditions and switch between properties
principle_rationale.37: Thermal Expansion — expand or contract resources based on load conditions
principle_rationale.36: Phase Transitions — transition between states based on threshold conditions
principle_rationale.28: Mechanics Substitution — use different interaction mechanisms under different conditions
principle_rationale.32: Color Changes — change visual or logical indicators based on conditions
principle_rationale.6: Universality — make the component serve both conditions through multi-function design

software_examples: Adaptive bitrate: high quality on fast connections, lower on slow
Graceful degradation: full features for capable clients, basic for limited
A/B testing: different behavior based on user cohort
Rate limiting: permissive under normal load, restrictive under heavy load

separation_principles.name: Separation between System Levels
separation_principles.description: The whole system has one property while its parts have the opposite. Value A at the macro/system level and value NOT-A at the micro/component level (or vice versa).
when_to_use: The contradiction exists between the system's emergent behavior and its component behavior
Individual parts need one property but the aggregate needs the opposite
The system can achieve macro-level properties from micro-level opposites

principle_rationale.5: Merging — combine simple parts to achieve complex emergent behavior
principle_rationale.33: Homogeneity — uniform parts create heterogeneous system behavior
principle_rationale.40: Composite Materials — combine different materials for dual properties
principle_rationale.1: Segmentation — individual segments have opposite property from the whole
principle_rationale.12: Equipotentiality — individual components reach equilibrium at system level
principle_rationale.26: Copying — copies at component level create redundancy at system level
principle_rationale.31: Porous Materials — porous at micro level, solid at macro level
principle_rationale.7: Nested Doll — nested components have opposite properties from container
principle_rationale.25: Self-service — components self-organize to produce system-level property

software_examples: Distributed system: eventually consistent globally, strongly consistent locally
Chain of responsibility: rigid individual handlers, flexible chain behavior
MapReduce: simple map operations, complex aggregate results
Blockchain: trustless individual nodes, trustworthy system consensus
